meaning: vt.使(痛苦等)易于忍受,减轻
examples: A number of measures were taken to alleviate the problem.
Feeling close to objects can alleviate loneliness.
We need to be aware of the risks and keep working to alleviate the dangers.
As with many financial issues, one way to alleviate some risk is through advanced financial planning.
And wealthy investors can use strategies to alleviate some of the effect of these tax increases.
